matrix_family: M_m = ((m,0,0),(0,0,-m),(0,m,-1)); eigenvalue moduli m, extent-ratio exactly m, anchor separation min|M_m v|_inf = m, irrational twist arccos(-1/2m)
build: amplify193.amplify_level (global-check, VERIFIED triple-free), seg_maxlen=2m+6, depth 3 (generations 1,2,3), seed 193, base_len 20, menu=candidate_step_vectors(2) size 124

triple_free_verified.note: first_disqualifier(chain) is None asserted at every level; sizes 716/1733/3219/5471 pts

dimension_d_logLambda_over_logm.trend: monotone toward 1.0 -> purer thread (matches design/lemma/exclusion sweep exactly)

OSC_covering_multiplicity.reading: match-scale multiplicity is ROCK-STABLE at [4,4,5] (cap 5) for every m>=4 -- the clean OSC signature; m=3 spikes to 6. Fine-scale (g1) full-diameter multiplicity DROPS with m (7->5). Bigger m => cleaner, tighter-capped OSC signature, not dramatically lower.

sibling_relative_gap_rho0_min.reading: local one-generation OSC quantity: bounded away from 0 (~0.25-0.40) for all m, no monotone degradation. Local finite OSC check stays clean at every m.

availability_proxy.definition: direct Ahlfors ratio C_d(r)=max_q c(q,r)/r^d and margin vs the Q-metric Mauldin-Williams bound 2*r^d; m=3 reference runs at ~0 margin (c(~4.44)=12 == 2*4.44^d threshold)

max_C_d_over_r_in_1..10.note: peak is a small-r (r=2) lattice artifact for all m

margin_vs_2rd_in_danger_window_r3to6.reading: m=6 is the ONLY m whose crowd sits UNDER the 2*r^d MW bound with POSITIVE slack across the whole core danger window r=3..6 (margin up to +0.77); m=3 is binding (~0), m=4/m=5 dip negative. Driven by d->1.035 (r^d larger) + slow crowd growth.

r_star_analog_1.48m.note: at the connector-reach analog the crowd tracks the C~2 bound for every m (d-regularity constant is ~2 universally); anisotropy leak makes Chebyshev margins slightly negative at large r

availability_proxy.connector_word_space: seg_maxlen 2m+6 = 12/14/16/18 over a fixed 124-symbol menu -> the raw per-stitch legal-word space grows exponentially with m; no jam at any m (feasibility never lost)

verdict.bigger_m_opens_margin: MODESTLY YES. The d-regularity constant stays ~2 for every m, so bigger m does NOT transform raw crowding. But d->1.035, the match-scale multiplicity cap tightens 6->5 and stays g-stable, fine-scale full-diameter multiplicity drops (7->5), and m=6 is the first matrix with genuinely POSITIVE availability slack (crowd < 2*r^d) across the danger window r=3..6. Sibling gap stays ~0.3 (local OSC intact) at every m.
verdict.cleaner_OSC_signature: YES for bigger m: match-scale mult is a flat, tightly-capped [4,4,5] (cap 5) for m>=4 vs m=3's [3,6,5] (cap 6); fine scales cleaner.
verdict.pick_the_provable_matrix: m=6 (with m=5 as a balanced alternative). m=6 has the purest thread (d=1.035), the cleanest & tightest-capped covering multiplicity ([4,4,5], full-diam g1=5), and the ONLY positive availability margin in the danger window. Multiplicity is uniformly bounded (cap 5) for all m, so 'bounded mult' is equally plausible to prove for any m -- m=6 just gives the most slack.
verdict.caveat: The win over m=3 is modest, not dramatic. And bigger m has a LONGER connector alphabet (seg_maxlen 18 for m=6), so the MENU-CLOSURE graph (all legal stitch transitions) that the actual open OSC proof must bound is COMBINATORIALLY LARGER -- the realized-walk signature is cleaner but the finite menu-closure certificate to discharge is heavier. 'Easier OSC' holds for the measured/realized multiplicity, not automatically for the menu-closure formalization.
